remember: Students will be able to recall and list the basic syntax of SQL commands.
Students will be able to identify the main components of a SQL query.
Students will be able to recall and use SQL data types.
understand: Students will be able to understand the basic concepts of SQL and its importance in database management.
Students will be able to comprehend the difference between various SQL commands (e.g., SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE).
Students will be able to explain the concept of SQL tables, rows, and columns.
apply: Students will be able to write and execute simple SQL queries to retrieve data from a database.
Students will be able to use SQL commands to perform basic database operations (e.g., insert, update, delete).
Students will be able to create and manipulate SQL tables, including adding, dropping, and altering tables.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze and optimize SQL queries for better performance.
Students will be able to identify and troubleshoot common SQL errors and exceptions.
Students will be able to design and implement a simple database schema using SQL.

ncert_objectives: To understand the basic syntax of SQL commands and their uses.
To learn how to write and execute SQL queries to retrieve data from a database.
To understand the concept of SQL tables, rows, and columns.
board_exam_objectives: To write and execute SQL queries to retrieve data from a database.
To use SQL commands to perform basic database operations.
To create and manipulate SQL tables.
